topic: NLP4PBM: A Systematic Review on Process Extraction using Natural Language Processing with Rule-based, Machine and Deep Learning Methods
topic_definition: Stage-split operational criteria for this review. Stage 1 is title+abstract screening only: decide by observable evidence of the core task (text-to-process extraction) and core method role (NLP/text processing is central to extraction, not peripheral). If abstract-level evidence indicates core fit but full details are missing, keep as maybe for Stage 2. Stage 2 is fulltext confirmation: enforce complete eligibility (paper type, language/fulltext availability, primary research, concrete method completeness, and empirical validation).
summary: Parsed screening criteria from criteria_corrected_3papers/2409.13738.md; operationalized into Stage 1 observable projection and Stage 2 confirmatory projection.

summary_topics.description: NLP4PBM: A Systematic Review on Process Extraction using Natural Language Processing with Rule-based, Machine and Deep Learning Methods

required.criterion: [Stage 1 observable gate] Core task fit: title/abstract should indicate extraction or generation of a structured process representation/model (for example BPMN, DMN, declarative process model, process graph) from natural language text.

[Stage 1 observable gate] Method-role fit: NLP/text processing must be central to the extraction pipeline (for example parsing, information extraction, semantic analysis, sequence labeling, relation extraction in service of process-model construction), not a background utility.

[Stage 1 observable gate] Uncertainty handling: if core task + method-role evidence exists but abstract does not fully expose experiment design or implementation depth, keep for Stage 2 (maybe) rather than hard exclusion.
[Stage 2 confirmatory gate] Full research article / full research paper.
[Stage 2 confirmatory gate] Full text available and written in English.
[Stage 2 confirmatory gate] Primary research (original contribution; not secondary research).
[Stage 2 confirmatory gate] Concrete extraction method is sufficiently specified in fulltext (not only conceptual discussion).
[Stage 2 confirmatory gate] Empirical validation is described in fulltext (experiments/evaluation supporting the extraction method).

exclusion_criteria.criterion: [Stage 1 strong negative] Topic-adjacent or process-adjacent only: paper discusses BPM/process domain but not the core objective of extracting/constructing process representation from natural language text.

[Stage 1 strong negative] Output-object mismatch: work focuses only on generic IE/NER/relation extraction/classification/retrieval/question answering/sentiment without explicit mapping to process representation/model construction.
[Stage 1 strong negative] Generic NLP/LLM/dataset/foundation-model paper with no explicit text-to-process extraction task should be excluded.

[Stage 1 strong negative] Non-target BPM task: process redesign, matching, prediction/forecasting, compliance monitoring, recommendation, simulation, or natural-text generation from process models without core extraction target.
[Stage 2 only negative] Not a full research paper, such as poster/short paper/abstract/report/thesis/book chapter; or secondary research (survey/review/meta-analysis/comments).
[Stage 2 only negative] No full text available or not written in English.
[Stage 2 only negative] Method is only superficial/conceptual and does not provide an executable extraction pipeline.
[Stage 2 only negative] Does not describe empirical validation for the extraction method.

stage1_observable.scope: Title+abstract only; recall-preserving gate for core semantic fit.
strong_positive_evidence: Explicit text-to-process objective (extract/generate process model/representation from natural language)
Process-model target object stated (BPMN/DMN/declarative process/process graph/workflow model)
NLP/text processing explicitly used to construct the process representation
weak_or_unclear_evidence: Core process-extraction intent is plausible but experiment/method depth is not explicit in abstract
Insufficient abstract detail on validation should defer to Stage 2 instead of hard exclusion
strong_negative_evidence: Process/BPM topic without text-to-process extraction objective
Only entity/relation/classification/retrieval tasks without explicit process-representation construction
BPM tasks outside extraction target (prediction/redesign/compliance/matching/simulation/text generation from process)

required_checks: Full research paper and primary research

negative_overrides: Topic-adjacent but non-extraction papers should not be retained by topical similarity alone
Process-adjacent papers without process-representation target object are out-of-scope
Mentioning modeling/extraction/formalization terms is insufficient unless tied to text-to-process core objective
